steps.title: Use the context clues
steps.content: The text calls the Wikipedia entries hoaxes and fictitious entries. It also says they were eventually recognized as something and deleted. The blank therefore needs a noun for made-up false information, not a neutral or positive kind of writing.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong meanings
steps.content: Choice A means improvements, which conflicts with entries that were deleted as hoaxes. Choice B means examinations or interpretations, but the entries themselves were fake subjects, not analyses of real subjects. Choice D means disclosures of truth, the opposite of invented misinformation.

steps.title: Choose the precise word
steps.content: Fabrications are invented falsehoods presented as real. That meaning matches fictitious Wikipedia entries for a made-up band and a made-up general that remained on the site until they were recognized and removed. The answer is C.
